title: Methods and apparatus to analyze emissions for industry operations
abstract: Methods and apparatus to analyze emissions for industry operations include obtaining a definition of at least one of a first part, product, activity or usage, or service. Applicable emissions and activity data for the at least one of the first part, product, activity or usage, or service are identified. One or more differences between the at least one of the first part, product, activity or usage, or service and at least one of a second part, product, activity or usage, or service for which the applicable emissions data was collected are determined. Emissions for the at least one of the first part, product, activity or usage, or service are estimated by modifying the applicable emissions data based on the one or more differences, and the estimate emissions are presented.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method for obtaining an emissions estimate, the method comprising: obtaining a definition of at least one of a first part, a first product, a first usage, or a first service; identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based upon the definition; estimating emissions for the at least one of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based upon the applicable data; presenting the estimated emissions to a screen via a graphical interface; and performing an action in response to the estimated emissions. 2. The method of claim 1. wherein obtaining a definition of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: inputting the definition of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service via the graphical interface; or obtaining the definition of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service from design data stored on an activity database. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises selecting at least one life cycle phase for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service via the graphical interface. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein selecting a life cycle phase for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises selecting at least one of the following life cycle phases: a raw materials acquisition phase; a processing phase; a manufacturing phase; a transportation phase; a usage phase; a maintenance phase; a remanufacturing phase; or 

 an end-of-life phase. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein estimating emissions for the at least one of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises estimating emissions across the at least one selected life cycle phase for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service to provide an absolute emissions value. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises selecting emission factors from emissions data provided by a supplier stored in a primary factor database and from publicly available or industry 7 standard emissions data stored in a secondary factor database. 7. The method of claim 3. wherein estimating emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: determining one or more differences over the at least one selected life cycle phase between the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service and a second part, a second product, a second usage, or a second service; and estimating the emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on the determined differences. 8. The method of claim 3, wherein presenting the estimated emissions to a screen via a graphical interface comprising presenting the estimated emissions according to the at least one selected life cycle phase. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising estimating a total emissions related to the first product across a plurality of life cycle phases related to the first product, wherein the total emissions are defined by: Total Emissions = EE X fy + EF 2 t 2 + EF 3 X t 3 + EF 4 X t 4 + EF n X t n , wherein EFn is an emission factor corresponding an activity and tn is a duration of the activity. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein performing an action in response to the estimated emissions comprises replacing the first part or the first product, improving the first part or the first product, discontinuing the first usage or the first service, adjusting the first usage or the 

first service, or implementing the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service into a facility. 11. A computing system, comprising: one or more processors; an emissions factor database storing emissions data related to at least one of a first part, a first product, a first usage, or a first service communicated to the one or more processors; an activity database storing activities data related to the first part, first product, first usage, or first service communicated to the one or more processors; a memory system comprising one or more non-transitory computer-readable media storing instructions that, when executed by at least one of the one or more processors, cause the computing system to perform operations, the operations comprising: obtaining a definition of at least one of a first part, a first product, a first usage, or a first service; identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based upon the definition; estimating emissions for the at least one of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based upon the applicable data; presenting the estimated emissions to a screen; and performing an action in response to the estimated emissions. 12. The computing system of claim 11. wherein identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: identify ing applicable emissions data from the emissions factor database; or identifying applicable activities data from the activity database. 13. The computing system of claim 11 , wherein the emissions factor database comprises: a primary factor database storing emissions data provided by a supplier related to the definition of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service; and a secondary factor database storing publicly available emissions data and/or industry standard emissions data related to the definition of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service. 14. The computing system of claim 11, wherein activity database comprises: a manufacturing database storing data related to the manufacture and/or remanufacturing of the first part or the first product: a supply chain database storing data related to the supply, procurement, and/or transport of the first part or the first product; a usage database storing data related the first usage or first service; a maintenance database related to the maintenance and/or end-of-life activities for the first part or the first product; or design data input into the computing system by a user via a graphical interface. 15. The computing system of claim 11, wherein the emissions factor database and the activity database are communicated to the one or more processors via a network. 16. The computing system of claim 11, wherein identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: identifying applicable emissions factor data related to the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service stored in the emissions factor database; and identifying applicable activities data related to the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service stored in the activity database. 17. The computing system of claim 16, wherein estimating emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises estimating an absolute emissions value for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on the emissions factor data identified with the emissions factor database and the activities data identified within the activity database. 18. The computing system of claim 16, wherein estimating emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: determining one or more differences between the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service and a second part, a second product, a second usage, or a second service; and estimating the emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on the determined differences. 19. The computing system of claim 12, further comprising a data collector communicated to the one or more processors, the data collector storing the emissions data identified from the emissions factor database and the activities data identified from the activity database 20. A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a computing system, cause the computing system to perform operations, the operations comprising: obtaining a definition of at least one of a first part, a first product, a first usage, or a first service; identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based upon the definition, wherein identifying applicable data comprises identifying applicable emissions factors or activities data, wherein identifying applicable data for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: identify ing applicable emissions factor data related to the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first sendee stored in an emissions factor database; and identifying applicable activities data related to the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service stored in an activity database; estimating emissions for the at least one of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first sen ice based upon the applicable data, wherein estimating emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service comprises: estimating an absolute emissions value for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on the identified applicable emissions factor data and activities data; or estimating an emissions value for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on a comparison with at least one of a second part, a second product, a second usage, or a second service that is related to the definition of the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service, wherein estimating an emissions value for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on a comparison with at least one of a second part, a second product, a second usage, or a second service compnses: estimating the emissions for the second part, the second product, the second usage, or the second service; 

 determining one or more differences between the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service and the second part, the second product, the second usage, or the second service; and estimating the emissions for the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first service based on the determined differences; presenting the estimated emissions to a screen via the graphical interface; and performing an action in response to the estimated emissions, wherein performing a action in response to the estimated emissions comprises replacing the first part or the first product, improving the first part or the first product, discontinuing the first usage or the first service, adjusting the first usage or the first sendee, or implementing the first part, the first product, the first usage, or the first sendee into a facility.
